transcript.clean_text: it was time for the opening road stage of latoya roundi stage one would take us from le grand berroche to the hilltop town of homo over 178 hilly kilometers just under 3000 meters of elevation gain and four categorized climbs culminating with two large circuits before the climb to the finish in the ancient town of honor five breakaway riders went away early in the day after a very fast start julius johansson thomas champion tim naberman antoine d ball and valerie debbo of the swiss national team and meanwhile the chase the pacemaking was being done by the ineos grenville's looking after their race leader ethan hayter here in the green jersey after winning the prologue time trial yesterday well first blood in the king of the mountains went to the man from confidence thomas champion actually won the first three climbs of the day and would move into the lead in that classification at the end of the day pacemaking still being made by the ineos grenadiers as the kilometers continue to tick by in beautiful sunshine and the man doing a lot of the work today was lauren's depose the belgian feeling very very good indeed and then with just under 40 kilometers to go there was an acceleration from antoine at the above taking with him thomas champion in pursuit of yet more king of the mountains points and with just under 30 kilometers to go despite the combined efforts of both of these riders their tenure at the front was to come to an end the 20 k's ago was still the ineos grenadiers augmented by efforts from yumbo bisma and also getting into the mix fernanda gaviria of uae team emirates but with just under 14 kilometers to go there was catastrophe a big crash at the back end of the peloton taking down the race leader ethan hater he was to take no further part in the finale then there was a big attack from this man the french national road champion remy cavania surged clear with five k's to go for quick step alpha vinyl but he could not open a lead of more than eight seconds and with just on two kilometers to go the remnants of the peloton brought him back to heel before the final ascent of the ferociously steep climb to hormone the first rider to go was brandon mcnulty and the white there of uae team emirates but he was closely pursued by a very much informed rowan dennis obviously in the orange jersey of points leader and he went very very long indeed using that superb ttf of his coming in to the final vote he was being pursued by dylan turns the flesh on winner and just inside to the line it was turns who took victory stole victory in fact from rowan dennis for another win the belgian absolutely flying but the consolation prize for rowan dennis was that he was to move in to the lead of this race dylan turns took the stage ahead of dennis hershey blast off her man's o'connor caruso and the rest delight again from the man from wallonia ron dennis leads this race heading into stage two builds crosshardnet up into second place guarantomas moves into third turns up to fourth place so the man in green as we head into stage two of what has been a very exciting race so far will be rowan dennis of jumbo bisma the next stage don't miss it it should be an absolute blinder finishing in ishal over 168.8 rolling kilometers see you then
